title: Curaleaf Reports First Quarter 2024 Results
text: Curaleaf achieved Q1 2024 net revenue of $338.9 million, representing a modest 2% year-over-year increase. The company maintained an adjusted gross margin of 48%, up 120 basis points sequentially, while adjusted EBITDA reached $76.7 million at a 23% margin—a 40 basis point improvement compared to the prior year. The organization generated $46.1 million in operating cash flow and $33.1 million in free cash flow from continuing operations during the quarter. Cash reserves stood at $105 million at quarter-end, with outstanding debt at $580.3 million net of unamortized discounts. The company expanded its U.S. retail footprint to 145 stores, including its first adult-use location in New York's Hudson Valley. Product innovations included launching Stir by Zero Proof beverages in Illinois and Stiq vape cartridges in Arizona. Internationally, Curaleaf strengthened its position through acquiring Northern Green Canada for approximately $16 million plus earnout considerations, establishing EU-GMP certified production capacity. The company also completed its first wholesale shipment into the Czech Republic and acquired Can4Med, a Polish pharmaceutical wholesaler. Executive Chairman Boris Jordan characterized the quarter as an important inflection point, noting the company's evolution from an MSO (multi-state operator) to an MCO (multi-country operator) spanning 15+ countries globally.
